title: Self-Controlled Case Series (SCCS)
description: SCCS is a within-person study design used for drug safety surveillance. Each patient serves as their own control, eliminating time-invariant confounding. You define an exposure cohort, an outcome cohort, and one or more risk windows to estimate the relative incidence of the outcome during exposed versus unexposed time periods.
tips: Select an exposure cohort (e.g., a drug of interest) and an outcome cohort (e.g., an adverse event) from your existing cohort definitions.
Configure risk windows with start/end offsets anchored to era start or era end to capture the biologically plausible risk period.
Set a naive period (default 365 days) in Study Population settings to ensure patients have sufficient observation time before first exposure.
Enable 'First outcome only' to restrict the analysis to patients' first occurrence of the outcome, which is standard for acute events.
Choose between simple and weighted SCCS models depending on whether you need to account for event-dependent observation periods.
Add covariate settings for conditions, drugs, or procedures to adjust for time-varying confounders within the observation period.
After saving the design, select a CDM data source and click Execute to run the analysis against real patient data.
Review execution history in the Results tab to compare runs across different data sources or parameter configurations.
